case.prediction_format: Return ranked root causes as objects with `time` as Unix seconds, `component` as a candidate root cause service, and `reason` as a candidate root cause metric.

reasoner.analysis: Checked 0-180s metrics. Checkoutservice latency.p90 dev=+1.7 and paymentservice cpu dev=+2.5 show initial anomalies. Implication: checkoutservice latency may drive downstream issues. Ranking top1=checkoutservice_latency

reasoner.analysis: Checked 60-180s checkoutservice metrics. Latency.p90 dev=+1.7 and workload increase confirm latency as root cause. Traces show internal latency but no external anomalies. Ranking remains checkoutservice_latency first.

reasoner.analysis: Checked 90-120s paymentservice metrics. CPU and latency show normal or decreased values. Workload increase is minor. Traces show normal call patterns. No significant anomalies validate paymentservice as root cause. Ranking remains checkoutservice_latency first.
Checkoutservice latency.p90 anomalies (dev=+80-+142) dominate. Workload and CPU show minor spikes. Productcatalogservice latency follows as secondary. Final ranking reflects metrics-led hypothesis.
